**class _AnalyzeJavaFeatureTool(_BaseBedrockArchitectTool):**
  `def _run(self, feature_data: str) -> str`

**class _ApplySmartAssumptionTool(_BaseBedrockArchitectTool):**
  `def _run(self, assumption_data: str) -> str`

**class _CreateConversionPlanTool(_BaseBedrockArchitectTool):**
  `def _run(self, plan_data: Any) -> str`

**class _GetAssumptionConflictsTool(_BaseBedrockArchitectTool):**
  `def _run(self, conflict_data: str) -> str`

**class _ValidateBedrockCompatibilityTool(_BaseBedrockArchitectTool):**
  `def _run(self, compatibility_data: str) -> str`

**class _GenerateBlockDefinitionsTool(_BaseBedrockArchitectTool):**
  `def _run(self, block_data: str) -> str`

**class _GenerateItemDefinitionsTool(_BaseBedrockArchitectTool):**
  `def _run(self, item_data: str) -> str`

**class _GenerateRecipeDefinitionsTool(_BaseBedrockArchitectTool):**
  `def _run(self, recipe_data: str) -> str`

**class _GenerateEntityDefinitionsTool(_BaseBedrockArchitectTool):**
  `def _run(self, entity_data: str) -> str`

**class _CreateLlmConversionPlanTool(_BaseBedrockArchitectTool):**
  `def _run(self, plan_data: str) -> str`

### `ai-engine/agents/bedrock_builder.py`
> Bedrock Builder Agent for generating Bedrock add-on files from Java mod analysis.
*deps: PIL, jinja2, langchain_core.tools, models.smart_assumptions, pydantic, templates.template_engine, utils.atlas_descriptor_parser, zipfile*

**class BedrockBuilderAgent:**
  *Bedrock Builder Agent responsible for generating Bedrock add-on files*
  `def __init__(self)`
  `def get_instance(cls)`
    *Get singleton instance of BedrockBuilderAgent*
  `def get_tools(self) -> List`
    *Get tools available to this agent*
  `def build_block_addon_mvp(self, registry_name: str, texture_path: str, jar_path: str, output_dir: str) -> Dict[str, Any]`
    *MVP-focused method to build Bedrock add-on from JavaAnalyzerAgent output.*
  `def build_entity_addon_mvp(self, entities: list, jar_path: str, output_dir: str) -> Dict[str, Any]`
    *MVP method to build Bedrock add-on structure for entity-only mods.*
  `def _build_bp_mvp(self, bp_path: Path, namespace: str, block_name: str, bp_uuid: str) -> List[str]`
    *Build behavior pack for MVP.*
  `def _build_rp_mvp(self, rp_path: Path, namespace: str, block_name: str, rp_uuid: str, texture_path: str, jar_path: str) -> List[str]`
    *Build resource pack for MVP.*
  `def _copy_texture_mvp(self, rp_path: Path, block_name: str, texture_path: str, jar_path: str) -> List[str]`
    *Copy and resize texture from JAR to resource pack.*
  `def _package_addon_mvp(self, temp_path: Path, addon_path: Path) -> None`
    *Package BP and RP into .mcaddon file.*
  `def _extract_all_textures_from_jar(self, jar_path: str, rp_path: Path, namespace: str) -> Dict[str, Any]`
    *Bulk extract all textures from JAR's assets/*/textures/ directories.*
  `def _extract_atlas_textures_from_jar(self, jar_path: str, rp_path: Path, namespace: str) -> Dict[str, Any]`
    *Extract textures from sprite sheet atlases using JSON descriptors.*
  `def _map_java_texture_to_bedrock(self, java_path: str) -> str`
    *Map Java mod texture path to Bedrock resource pack texture path.*
  `def _map_texture_type_to_bedrock(self, java_type: str) -> str`
    *Map Java texture type to Bedrock texture type (plural form).*
  `def _create_bp_manifest(self, analysis_data)`
    *Legacy method for compatibility.*
  `def _create_bp_blocks(self, analysis_data)`
    *Legacy method for compatibility.*
  `def _create_rp_manifest(self, analysis_data)`
    *Legacy method for compatibility.*
  `def _create_rp_blocks(self, analysis_data)`
    *Legacy method for compatibility.*
  `def _copy_textures(self, analysis_data)`
    *Legacy method for compatibility.*
  `def _build_bedrock_structure(structure_data: str) -> str`
    *Build basic Bedrock addon structure.*
  `def _generate_block_definitions(block_data: str) -> str`
    *Generate Bedrock block definition files.*
  `def _convert_assets(asset_data: str) -> str`
    *Convert assets to Bedrock format.*
  `def _package_addon(package_data: str) -> str`
    *Package addon into .mcaddon file.*

**class _BuildBedrockStructureInput(BaseModel):**
  *Args for :class:`_BuildBedrockStructureTool`.*

**class _GenerateBlockDefinitionsInput(BaseModel):**
  *Args for :class:`_GenerateBlockDefinitionsTool`.*

**class _ConvertAssetsInput(BaseModel):**
  *Args for :class:`_ConvertAssetsTool`.*

**class _PackageAddonInput(BaseModel):**
  *Args for :class:`_PackageAddonTool`.*

**class _BaseBedrockBuilderTool(BaseTool):**
  *Common scaffolding for Bedrock Builder typed tool wrappers.*

**class _BuildBedrockStructureTool(_BaseBedrockBuilderTool):**
  `def _run(self, structure_data: str) -> str`

**class _GenerateBlockDefinitionsTool(_BaseBedrockBuilderTool):**
  `def _run(self, block_data: str) -> str`

**class _ConvertAssetsTool(_BaseBedrockBuilderTool):**
  `def _run(self, asset_data: str) -> str`

**class _PackageAddonTool(_BaseBedrockBuilderTool):**
  `def _run(self, package_data: str) -> str`

**class ManifestData:**

  *Generator for Bedrock add-on manifest.json files.*

  `def generate_manifests(self, mod_data: Dict[str, Any]) -> Tuple[Dict[str, Any], Dict[str, Any]]`
    *Generate both behavior pack and resource pack manifests.*

    *Create behavior pack manifest.*
  `def _create_resource_manifest(self, name: str, description: str, version: List[int], pack_uuid: str, capabilities: List[str]) -> Dict[str, Any]`
    *Create resource pack manifest.*

    *Determine required Bedrock capabilities based on mod features.*

    *Add dependencies between behavior and resource packs.*

    *Validate manifest against Bedrock schema.*

    *Write manifests to disk in the appropriate pack directories.*
  `def generate_single_manifest(self, pack_type: PackType, mod_data: Dict[str, Any]) -> Dict[str, Any]`
    *Generate a single manifest for either behavior or resource pack.*

**class BlockItemGenerator:**
  *Generator for Bedrock block and item definition files.*
  `def __init__(self)`
  `def generate_blocks(self, java_blocks: List[Dict[str, Any]]) -> Dict[str, Any]`
    *Generate Bedrock block definitions from Java blocks.*
  `def generate_items(self, java_items: List[Dict[str, Any]]) -> Dict[str, Any]`
    *Generate Bedrock item definitions from Java items.*
  `def generate_recipes(self, java_recipes: List[Dict[str, Any]], namespace: str = 'mod') -> Dict[str, Any]`
    *Convert Java recipes to Bedrock format using RecipeConverterAgent.*
  `def generate_tool_item(self, data: Dict[str, Any]) -> Dict[str, Any]`
    *Generate a Bedrock tool item definition.*
  `def generate_armor_item(self, data: Dict[str, Any]) -> Dict[str, Any]`
    *Generate a Bedrock armor item definition.*
  `def generate_consumable_item(self, data: Dict[str, Any]) -> Dict[str, Any]`
    *Generate a Bedrock consumable item definition (food, potions).*
  `def generate_ranged_weapon_item(self, data: Dict[str, Any]) -> Dict[str, Any]`

    *Generate a Bedrock rare/special item definition with enchantments.*

    *Convert a single Java block to Bedrock format.*
  `def _convert_java_item(self, java_item: Dict[str, Any]) -> Dict[str, Any]`
    *Convert a single Java item to Bedrock format.*

    *Parse Java item properties.*
  `def _determine_block_category(self, java_block: Dict[str, Any]) -> Optional[str]`
    *Determine appropriate creative menu category for block.*
  `def _determine_item_category(self, java_item: Dict[str, Any]) -> Optional[str]`
    *Determine appropriate creative menu category for item.*
  `def _write_json_files(self, definitions: Dict[str, Any], directory: Path, written_files: List[Path]) -> None`
    *Helper method to write JSON definitions to a directory.*
  `def write_definitions_to_disk(self, blocks: Dict[str, Any], items: Dict[str, Any], recipes: Dict[str, Any], bp_path: Path, rp_path: Path) -> Dict[str, List[Path]]`
    *Write block, item, and recipe definitions to disk.*

    *Apply entity properties to Bedrock components.*

    *Build Bedrock behavior config from Java goal config.*
  `def _add_legacy_goal(self, components: Dict[str, Any], goal_type: str, priority: int, goal_config: Dict[str, Any])`
    *Handle legacy goal types not in goal_mappings.*

    *Generate loot table for an entity.*
  `def _generate_animation_controllers(self, java_entity: Dict[str, Any], entity_id: str) -> Optional[Dict[str, Any]]`
    *Generate animation controllers for an entity.*
  `def convert_ai_goals(self, java_goals: List[Dict[str, Any]]) -> Dict[str, Dict[str, Any]]`
    *Convert Java AI goals to Bedrock behaviors.*
  `def _generate_entity_behaviors(self, java_entity: Dict[str, Any]) -> Optional[Dict[str, Any]]`
    *Generate separate behavior file for complex entities.*
  `def _generate_entity_animations(self, java_entity: Dict[str, Any]) -> Optional[Dict[str, Any]]`
    *Generate animation definitions for entities.*
  `def write_entities_to_disk(self, entities: Dict[str, Any], bp_path: Path, rp_path: Path) -> Dict[str, List[Path]]`
    *Write entity definitions to disk.*

### `ai-engine/agents/fewshot_enhancer_agent.py`
> Few-Shot Enhancer Agent for PortKit
*deps: mmsd.premium_client, utils.logging_config*

**class EnhancementResult:**
  *Result from few-shot enhancement.*

**class FewShotEnhancerAgent:**
  *Few-shot enhancement agent that uses frontier AI models for initial conversion.*
  `def __init__(self, model: str = 'deepseek-v4-pro')`
    *Initialize the FewShotEnhancerAgent.*
  `def _get_client(self) -> Optional[PortKitPremium]`
    *Get or create OpenRouter client.*
  `def enhance(self, java_source: str, instruction: str, model: Optional[str] = None, use_fallback: bool = True) -> EnhancementResult`
    *Generate initial Bedrock conversion using few-shot prompting.*
  `def enhance_batch(self, conversions: List[Dict[str, str]], model: Optional[str] = None) -> List[EnhancementResult]`
    *Enhance multiple conversions in batch.*
  `def estimate_cost(self, instruction: str, java_source: str, model: Optional[str] = None) -> Dict[str, Any]`
    *Estimate API cost for an enhancement.*
  `def _estimate_quality(self, result: ConversionResult) -> float`
    *Estimate output quality based on completeness.*
  `def close(self)`
    *Close the client connection.*
  `def __enter__(self)`
  `def __exit__(self, *args)`

**class FewShotEnhancerTools:**
  *Tool wrapper for LangChain/LangGraph integration.*
  `def enhance_tool(java_source: str, instruction: str, model: str = 'deepseek-v4-pro') -> str`
    *LangChain tool for few-shot enhancement.*
  `def get_tools() -> List[callable]`
    *Return the list of tools for LangChain/LangGraph registration.*

### `ai-engine/agents/file_packager.py`
> File Packager for creating .mcaddon packages from Bedrock add-on components
*deps: magic, zipfile*

**class FilePackager:**
  *Packager for creating .mcaddon files from behavior and resource pack directories.*
  `def __init__(self)`
  `def package_addon(self, addon_data: Dict[str, Any]) -> Dict[str, Any]`
    *Create .mcaddon package from addon data.*
  `def _organize_pack_structure(self, temp_path: Path, behavior_pack_path: Optional[str], resource_pack_path: Optional[str], source_directories: List...) -> Dict[str, Any]`
    *Organize pack directories into proper Bedrock structure.*
  `def _is_behavior_pack(self, pack_path: Path) -> bool`
    *Determine if directory is a behavior pack.*
  `def _is_resource_pack(self, pack_path: Path) -> bool`
    *Determine if directory is a resource pack.*
  `def _get_pack_name(self, pack_path: Path, pack_type: str) -> str`
    *Get pack name from manifest or directory name.*
  `def _copy_pack_contents(self, source_path: Path, dest_path: Path)`
    *Copy pack contents with validation.*
  `def _validate_file(self, file_path: Path) -> bool`
    *Validate individual file against constraints.*
  `def _validate_pack_structure(self, temp_path: Path) -> Dict[str, Any]`
    *Validate the organized pack structure.*
  `def _validate_individual_pack(self, pack_dir: Path, pack_type: str) -> Dict[str, Any]`
    *Validate an individual pack directory.*
  `def _create_mcaddon_file(self, temp_path: Path, output_path: Path)`
    *Create the final .mcaddon ZIP file.*
  `def validate_mcaddon_file(self, mcaddon_path: Path) -> Dict[str, Any]`
    *Validate an existing .mcaddon file.*

### `ai-engine/agents/gameplay_comparison_agent.py`
> Direct Gameplay Comparison Agent

**class GameTestScript:**
  *Represents a test script to run in Minecraft.*

**class Screenshot:**
  *Represents a captured screenshot.*

**class GameplayComparisonResult:**
  *Results from comparing gameplay between Java and Bedrock.*

**class MinecraftLauncher:**
  *Handles launching Minecraft instances.*
  `def __init__(self, java_path: Optional[str] = None, bedrock_path: Optional[str] = None)`
  `def is_java_installed(self) -> bool`
    *Check if Java Minecraft is available.*
  `def is_bedrock_installed(self) -> bool`
    *Check if Bedrock Minecraft is available.*
  `def _check_java_command(self) -> bool`
    *Check if Minecraft launcher command is available.*
  `def launch_java(self, version: str = '1.20.4', username: str = 'TestBot') -> bool`
    *Launch Java Edition Minecraft.*
  `def launch_bedrock(self, version: str = '1.20.60') -> bool`
    *Launch Bedrock Edition Minecraft.*
  `def stop_instance(self, edition: str) -> None`
    *Stop a running Minecraft instance.*
  `def close_all(self) -> None`
    *Close all running instances.*

**class GameplayTestRunner:**
  *Runs automated test scripts in Minecraft.*
  `def __init__(self, screenshot_dir: str = '/tmp/minecraft_screenshots')`
  `def run_test_script(self, game_edition: str, script: GameTestScript, game_version: str) -> Dict[str, Any]`
    *Execute a test script and capture results.*
  `def _capture_screenshot(self, game_edition: str, test_name: str, game_version: str) -> Optional[Screenshot]`
    *Capture a screenshot from the game.*
  `def get_screenshots(self, game_edition: Optional[str] = None) -> List[Screenshot]`
    *Get all captured screenshots, optionally filtered by game edition.*

**class ScreenshotComparator:**
  *Compares screenshots to detect visual differences.*
  `def __init__(self, similarity_threshold: float = 0.85)`
  `def compare_screenshots(self, java_screenshot: Screenshot, bedrock_screenshot: Screenshot) -> Dict`
    *Compare two screenshots and return difference metrics.*
  `def generate_visual_diff(self, java_screenshot: Screenshot, bedrock_screenshot: Screenshot) -> bytes`
    *Generate a visual diff image between two screenshots.*
  `def compare_all_pairs(self, java_screenshots: List[Screenshot], bedrock_screenshots: List[Screenshot]) -> List[Dict]`
    *Compare all possible pairs of screenshots.*

**class GameplayComparisonAgent:**
  *Main agent for direct gameplay comparison between Java and Bedrock.*
  `def __init__(self, java_minecraft_path: Optional[str] = None, bedrock_minecraft_path: Optional[str] = None, screenshot_dir: str = ...)`
  `def _create_default_test_scripts(self) -> List[GameTestScript]`
    *Create default test scripts for validation.*
  `def validate_prerequisites(self) -> Dict[str, bool]`
    *Check if all prerequisites are met.*
  `def run_comparison(self, conversion_id: str, java_mod_path: str, bedrock_addon_path: str, custom_tests: Optional[List[GameTestScript]] =...) -> GameplayComparisonResult`
    *Run a complete gameplay comparison between Java mod and Bedrock addon.*
  `def _run_test_suite(self, edition: str, tests: List[GameTestScript], version: str) -> List[Dict]`
    *Run a suite of tests on a specific game edition.*
  `def _compare_results(self, java_results: List[Dict], bedrock_results: List[Dict]) -> List[Dict]`
    *Compare test results between editions.*
  `def _generate_report(self, conversion_id: str, java_results: List[Dict], bedrock_results: List[Dict], visual_diffs: List[Dict]) -> GameplayComparisonResult`
    *Generate the final comparison report.*
  `def _calculate_success_rate(self, results: List[Dict]) -> float`
    *Calculate success rate from test results.*
  `def _identify_missing_features(self, java_results: List[Dict], bedrock_results: List[Dict]) -> List[str]`
    *Identify features that work in Java but not Bedrock.*
  `def _generate_recommendations(self, java_results: List[Dict], bedrock_results: List[Dict], visual_diffs: List[Dict], score: float) -> List[str]`
    *Generate recommendations based on comparison results.*
  `def _create_error_result(self, conversion_id: str, error_message: str) -> GameplayComparisonResult`
    *Create an error result.*
  `def get_result(self, conversion_id: str) -> Optional[GameplayComparisonResult]`
    *Get a previous result by conversion ID.*
  `def export_report(self, result: GameplayComparisonResult, output_path: str) -> None`
    *Export the comparison report to a file.*

  *Handles JAR/ZIP extraction and analysis*
  `def __init__(self, feature_patterns: Dict[str, List[str]])`
  `def read_java_sources_from_jar(self, jar_path: str) -> List[tuple]`
    *Read all .java source files from a JAR.*
  `def extract_mod_info_from_jar(self, jar: zipfile.ZipFile, file_list: list) -> dict`
    *Extract mod information from metadata files*
  `def analyze_assets_from_jar(self, file_list: list) -> dict`
    *Analyze assets in the JAR file*
  `def find_block_texture(self, file_list: list) -> Optional[str]`
    *Find a block texture in the JAR file list.*
  `def extract_registry_name_from_jar(self, jar: zipfile.ZipFile, file_list: list) -> str`
    *Extract block registry name from JAR.*
  `def _extract_mod_id_from_metadata(self, jar: zipfile.ZipFile, file_list: list) -> Optional[str]`
    *Extract mod ID from metadata files.*
  `def _find_block_class_name(self, file_list: list) -> Optional[str]`
    *Find the main block class name from file paths.*
  `def _class_name_to_registry_name(self, class_name: str) -> str`
    *Convert Java class name to registry name format.*
  `def extract_registry_name_from_jar_simple(self, jar: zipfile.ZipFile, file_list: list) -> str`
    *Extract block registry name from JAR metadata (simple version).*

`def _snake_case(name: str) -> str`
  *Convert CamelCase to snake_case.*

### `ai-engine/agents/java_analyzer/feature_extractor.py`
> AST-based and bytecode-based feature extraction for Java mods
*deps: javassist, tree_sitter, tree_sitter_java, utils.logging_config*

**class FeatureExtractor:**
  *Extracts mod features from Java AST and bytecode*
  `def __init__(self, feature_patterns: Dict[str, List[str]])`
  `def extract_features_from_ast(self, tree: Dict) -> Dict`
    *Extract features from parsed Java AST (tree-sitter format).*
  `def extract_features_from_bytecode(self, class_info: Dict) -> Dict`
    *Extract mod features from bytecode-analyzed class information.*
  `def extract_features_from_class_name(self, class_name: str) -> Dict`
    *Extract features from a single class name (fallback for parse failures).*
  `def extract_features_from_classes(self, file_list: List[str]) -> Dict`
    *Extract features from class file names (fallback method).*
  `def analyze_bytecode_class(self, class_data: bytes, class_name: str) -> Dict`
    *Analyze a Java class file using Javassist.*
  `def analyze_classes_with_bytecode(self, jar, file_list: List[str]) -> Dict`
    *Analyze .class files in a JAR using bytecode analysis.*
  `def parse_java_source(self, source_code: str) -> Optional[Dict]`
    *Parse Java source code into an AST using tree-sitter.*
  `def _get_tree_sitter_parser(self)`
    *Get or create tree-sitter parser instance.*
  `def _tree_sitter_to_dict(self, node, error_count: int = 0) -> Dict[str, Any]`
    *Convert tree-sitter node to dictionary.*
  `def _parse_java_source_fallback(self, source_code: str) -> Optional[Dict]`
    *Fallback parsing for incomplete Java source code.*
  `def extract_mod_metadata_from_ast(self, tree: Dict) -> Dict`
    *Extract mod metadata from parsed Java AST.*
  `def analyze_dependencies_from_ast(self, tree: Dict) -> List[Dict]`
    *Analyze dependencies from parsed Java AST.*
  `def detect_reflection_in_mods(self, tree: Dict) -> Dict`
    *Detect reflection usage in mods through static analysis.*
  `def _extract_string_from_node(self, node: Dict) -> Optional[str]`
    *Extract string value from an AST node.*
  `def _find_nodes_by_type(self, node: Dict, target_type: str) -> List[Dict]`
    *Find all nodes of a specific type in tree-sitter AST.*
  `def _extract_class_info_from_ts(self, class_node: Dict) -> Dict`
    *Extract class information from tree-sitter class_declaration node.*
  `def _get_superclass_text(self, superclass_node: Dict) -> str`
    *Get superclass name from superclass node.*
  `def _extract_modifiers(self, modifiers_node: Dict) -> List[str]`
    *Extract modifier keywords.*
  `def _get_method_name(self, method_node: Dict) -> str`
    *Get method name from method_declaration node.*
  `def _get_method_parameters(self, method_node: Dict) -> List[str]`
    *Get method parameters from method_declaration node.*
  `def _extract_block_properties_from_ts(self, class_node: Dict) -> Dict`
    *Extract block properties from tree-sitter block class node.*
  `def _extract_block_properties_from_bytecode(self, class_info: Dict) -> Dict`
    *Extract block properties from bytecode-analyzed class.*
  `def _get_method_arguments(self, inv_node: Dict) -> List`
    *Extract arguments from method_invocation node.*
  `def _extract_sound_type(self, arg_node: Dict) -> str`
    *Extract sound type from method argument.*
  `def _collect_identifiers(self, node: Dict, parts: List)`
    *Recursively collect identifiers from field_access nodes.*
  `def _get_ts_method_name(self, inv_node: Dict) -> str`
    *Get method name from method_invocation node.*
  `def _get_ts_qualifier(self, node: Dict) -> str`
    *Get qualifier from field_access or method_invocation.*
  `def _get_ts_member(self, node: Dict) -> str`
    *Get member name from field_access node.*
  `def _extract_numeric_arg(self, arg_node: Dict) -> float`
    *Extract numeric value from an argument node.*
  `def _extract_annotation_data_ts(self, ann_node: Dict) -> Dict`
    *Extract annotation data from tree-sitter annotation node.*
  `def _extract_string_content(self, string_node: Dict) -> str`
    *Extract string content from string_literal node.*
  `def _get_import_path(self, imp_node: Dict) -> str`
    *Get import path from import_declaration node.*
  `def _get_scoped_identifier_parts(self, node: Dict) -> List[str]`
    *Get parts from scoped_identifier.*

`def _class_name_to_registry_name(class_name: str) -> str`
  *Convert Java class name to registry name format.*

`def _snake_case(name: str) -> str`
  *Convert CamelCase to snake_case.*
